title: Grease on Upholstery
stainChemistry: Grease on upholstery: thick fat (kitchen / cooking grease) that needs to be lifted before chemistry. Cushion batting saturation is the failure mode — never pour. Code tag (W / W-S / S / X) determines if water-based cleaning is allowed. Powder preload is universal-safe across all upholstery codes (no liquid contact). Pro uses POG / mineral spirits at controlled concentration on cloth + cold blot for residue. Home: powder + Dawn damp cloth (W/W-S only) + weighted extraction.
whyThisWorks: Cornstarch / fuller's earth pulls bulk grease up before any liquid touches the upholstery. Dawn on a damp cloth (W / W-S code only) emulsifies residual grease. Weighted dry towel extraction prevents batting saturation. Pro escalation for aged polymerized grease (mineral spirits or amyl acetate at controlled concentration on cloth).
defaultAssumption: Living-room couch / dining chair grease spill. W or W-S code = home protocol. S-only = solvent only, water-based Dawn is mismatched (pro). X = vacuum only, pro. Zip-off washable cushion cover = wash cover separately.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Find code tag (W / W-S / S / X). W or W-S proceed; S-only / X = pro. If grease is solid (cooled bacon grease), lift bulk with flat-edge plastic card. No scraping.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cornstarch, baking soda, or fuller's earth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cover grease with thick layer of powder. 30–60 min absorb. Vacuum off. Powder preload is the universal-safe step — works on every code.

spottingProtocol.agent: Dawn + Soft Brush on Damp Cloth (W / W-S Only)
spottingProtocol.technique: Surfactant on damp cloth, never poured
spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cool water, soft toothbrush, damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn in cool water on damp cloth (NEVER pour on upholstery). Work with soft toothbrush. Press 5–10 min. S-only code: skip Dawn step entirely (water mismatch with solvent-only upholstery).

spottingProtocol.technique: Citrus solvent on cloth, controlled tamp
spottingProtocol.instruction: For aged / polymerized grease, pro applies Goo Gone to a white cloth (NEVER pour). Tight blotting from edge inward. Ventilate.
Weighted dry towel 30 min × 2–3 rounds + overnight. Fan-dry. Aggressive extraction prevents grease from wicking back up from batting.

steps: 1. Code check + flat-edge lift solid bulk.
3. Dawn + soft toothbrush on damp cloth (W/W-S only).
4. Goo Gone or POG for aged grease (pro), tight blotting from edge in.
products: Goo Gone / POG (aged grease, pro on cloth)
warnings: NEVER pour solvent or water on upholstery — flood = batting saturation
NEVER hot water — sets grease into batting
NEVER hot dryer (if cushion cover) until fully clear
Aggressive extraction matters more than aggressive chemistry

homeSolutions.instruction: Find the upholstery tag (under cushion). W / W-S = full protocol. S-only = solvent cleaning, water-based Dawn won't work — pro. X = vacuum only, pro. The good news: powder preload works on every code — it's a dry technique. So step 1 (powder) is always safe.

homeSolutions.agent: Lift Solid Bulk — Flat Edge Only
homeSolutions.instruction: If the grease is solid (cooled bacon grease, hardened fat), use the FLAT edge of a plastic card to lift it. Don't scrape upholstery — flat-edge lift only.

Cover the grease spot with a thick layer of cornstarch, baking soda, or fuller's earth (best on grease, Amazon ~$10). 30 to 60 minutes — longer than for thin oil, grease takes time to wick. Vacuum off thoroughly with upholstery attachment. Most surface bulk comes off this way.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn on a Damp Cloth (W / W-S Code Only)
homeSolutions.instruction: If your code is W or W-S: work a drop of Dawn with cool water into a damp cloth, press on the spot with a soft toothbrush 5–10 minutes. Damp-cloth rinse. NEVER pour — flood = cushion batting saturation. If the code is S-only, skip this step and go to pro escalation.

Stack heavy dry towels on the spot, weight with a book, 30 minutes. Replace with fresh towels, repeat 2–3 times. Finish with fresh towels overnight. Fan-dry in the morning. Aggressive extraction prevents grease from wicking back up from batting as the cushion dries.

homeSolutions.agent: Pro for Aged Grease, Polymerized, or S-Only Code
homeSolutions.instruction: Take to a pro upholstery cleaner if: code is S-only / X, grease is aged with stiff/shiny film (polymerized), batting saturation suspected, or visible feel of grease remains after one full cycle. Tell them: "Grease on [W / W-S / S / X] upholstery, age [fresh / aged], home-treated with cornstarch preload + Dawn damp cloth + weighted extraction. No hot water, no pouring."

steps: 1. Code check. S / X = pro. Zip-off cover = wash separately.
2. Lift solid bulk with flat-edge plastic card.
4. Dawn on damp cloth (W/W-S only), 5–10 min. NEVER pour.
5. Aggressive weighted dry towels × 2–3 + overnight + fan.
6. Aged / S-code / persistent = pro low-moisture extraction.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if upholstery code is S-only or X, if grease is aged + polymerized, or if cushion batting saturation suspected.
warnings: NEVER pour anything on upholstery — flood = batting saturation
NEVER hot water — sets grease into batting
Powder preload is universal-safe; Dawn is W/W-S only
Aggressive extraction matters more than aggressive chemistry

neverDo: Never pour solvent or water on upholstery
Never hot water on grease + upholstery
Never DIY solvent escalation on upholstery
fiberSensitivities: W / W-S upholstery: tolerates Dawn damp cloth
S-only: solvent cleaning, water-based mismatched — pro
Microfiber: tolerates Dawn but care with wetting

materialWarnings: Batting saturation is the upholstery ceiling

escalation.when: S-only / X code, aged polymerized grease, batting saturation suspected, persistent grease film after one full home cycle.
escalation.whatToTell: "Grease on [W / W-S / S / X] upholstery, age [fresh / aged / polymerized], home-treated with cornstarch preload + Dawn damp cloth + weighted extraction. No pouring, no hot water. [Residual greasy feel / batting saturation / aged film]."
escalation.specialistType: professional upholstery cleaner with low-moisture extraction

contextQuestions: Upholstery code — W / W-S / S / X?
Type of grease — kitchen / cooking / motor?
Has anyone applied solvent or hot water?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Motor oil = separate protocol. Aged polymerized grease = pro from minute one. Pre-applied solvent on home wool/cotton playbook DESTROYS upholstery (especially S-code).
